var_functions.query_db:5.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.query_db:5.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.query_db:5.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import defaultdict

# Direct access to the business data
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']
print('Total businesses loaded:', len(businesses))

# Extract state from description and identify businesses with WiFi
business_wifi_states = []
for biz in businesses:
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    
    # Extract state (2-letter code) from description
    # Pattern: "in City, ST," or similar
    state_match = re.search(r'\b([A-Z]{2})\b', desc)
    state = state_match.group(1) if state_match else 'Unknown'
    
    # Check if WiFi is available
    attrs = biz.get('attributes', {})
    wifi_available = False
    
    if isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi = attrs.get('WiFi', '')
        # WiFi values: "u'free'", "u'no'", "u'paid'", or possibly other values
        if wifi and 'no' not in str(wifi).lower():
            wifi_available = True
    
    business_wifi_states.append({
        'business_id': biz['business_id'],
        'state': state,
        'wifi_available': wifi_available
    })

print('Sample businesses with state and WiFi:')
for item in business_wifi_states[:10]:
    print(item)

args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import defaultdict

# Direct access to the business data
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']

# Extract state from description and identify businesses with WiFi
business_wifi_states = []
for biz in businesses:
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    
    # Extract state (2-letter code) from description
    state_match = re.search(r'\b([A-Z]{2})\b', desc)
    state = state_match.group(1) if state_match else 'Unknown'
    
    # Check if WiFi is available
    attrs = biz.get('attributes', {})
    wifi_available = False
    
    if isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi = attrs.get('WiFi', '')
        # WiFi values: "u'free'", "u'no'", "u'paid'", or possibly other values
        if wifi and 'no' not in str(wifi).lower():
            wifi_available = True
    
    business_wifi_states.append({
        'business_id': biz['business_id'],
        'state': state,
        'wifi_available': wifi_available
    })

# Count businesses with WiFi per state
state_counts = defaultdict(int)
for item in business_wifi_states:
    if item['wifi_available']:
        state_counts[item['state']] += 1

print('__RESULT__:')

val_args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import defaultdict

# Direct access to the business data
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']

# Extract state from description and identify businesses with WiFi
business_wifi_states = []
for biz in businesses:
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    
    # Extract state (2-letter code) from description
    state_match = re.search(r'\b([A-Z]{2})\b', desc)
    state = state_match.group(1) if state_match else 'Unknown'
    
    # Check if WiFi is available
    attrs = biz.get('attributes', {})
    wifi_available = False
    
    if isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi = attrs.get('WiFi', '')
        # WiFi values: "u'free'", "u'no'", "u'paid'", or possibly other values
        if wifi and 'no' not in str(wifi).lower():
            wifi_available = True
    
    business_wifi_states.append({
        'business_id': biz['business_id'],
        'state': state,
        'wifi_available': wifi_available
    })

# Count businesses with WiFi per state
state_counts = defaultdict(int)
for item in business_wifi_states:
    if item['wifi_available']:
        state_counts[item['state']] += 1

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_businesses': len(businesses),
    'businesses_with_wifi_count': len([b for b in business_wifi_states if b['wifi_available']]),
    'state_counts': dict(state_counts),
    'sample_businesses': business_wifi_states[:10]
}, default=str))

args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import defaultdict

# Get all businesses from the query result
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:38']

# Debug: print info about the data
print('Total businesses:', len(businesses))
print('Sample business:', businesses[0])
print('Business ID format:', businesses[0]['business_id'])

# Extract state from description and identify businesses with WiFi
business_wifi_states = []
state_pattern = re.compile(r'\b([A-Z]{2})\b')

for biz in businesses:
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    
    # Extract state (2-letter code) from description
    state_match = state_pattern.search(desc)
    state = state_match.group(1) if state_match else 'Unknown'
    
    # Check if WiFi is available
    attrs = biz.get('attributes', {})
    wifi_available = False
    
    if isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi = attrs.get('WiFi', '')
        # Check if WiFi exists and is not 'no'
        if wifi and 'no' not in str(wifi).lower():
            wifi_available = True
    
    business_wifi_states.append({
        'business_id': biz['business_id'],
        'state': state,
        'wifi_available': wifi_available,
        'review_count': biz.get('review_count', '0')
    })

# Count businesses with WiFi per state
state_counts = defaultdict(int)
for item in business_wifi_states:
    if item['wifi_available']:
        state_counts[item['state']] += 1

# Find state with highest count
if state_counts:
    max_state = max(state_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1])
else:
    max_state = ('None', 0)

print('State counts:', dict(state_counts))
print('Max state:', max_state)

# Print result in required format
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_businesses': len(businesses),
    'businesses_with_wifi_data': business_wifi_states,
    'state_counts': dict(state_counts),
    'max_state': max_state
}, default=str))

var_functions.query_db:5.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

He was very stern with me about explaining that to them because they are so short staffed which took me aback a little, but I also understand it was my fault for not specifying. args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import defaultdict

# Load all businesses
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:44']

# Debug: Check how many businesses we have
print('Total businesses:', len(businesses))

# Check all attributes to find WiFi patterns
wifi_businesses = []
for biz in businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes', {})
    if isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi = attrs.get('WiFi', '')
        if wifi:
            print(f"Business {biz['business_id']} has WiFi: {wifi}")
        if wifi and 'no' not in str(wifi).lower():
            print(f"  -> This one HAS WiFi available!")
            wifi_businesses.append(biz)

print('\nTotal businesses with WiFi:', len(wifi_businesses))

var_functions.query_db:5.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.query_db:5.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

result.preview: [{"business_id": "businessid_49", "state": "CA", "wifi_available": false, "wifi_value": "u'no'"}, {"business_id": "businessid_47", "state": "MO", "wifi_available": false, "wifi_value": ""}, {"business_id": "businessid_88", "state": "ID", "wifi_available": false, "wifi_value": ""}, {"business_id": "businessid_41", "state": "FL", "wifi_available": false, "wifi_value": null}, {"business_id": "businessid_33", "state": "IN", "wifi_available": false, "wifi_value": ""}]

args.code:
import json
import re

# Load all businesses
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:44']

# Debug print
print('Total businesses:', len(businesses))

# Process each business to extract state and WiFi
results = []
regex = re.compile(r'in [^,]+, ([A-Z]{2}),')

for biz in businesses:
    # Extract state
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    state_match = regex.search(desc)
    state = state_match.group(1) if state_match else 'Unknown'
    
    # Check WiFi
    attrs = biz.get('attributes', {})
    wifi_available = False
    wifi_value = None
    
    if isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi_value = attrs.get('WiFi', '')
        if wifi_value and 'no' not in wifi_value.lower():
            wifi_available = True
    
    results.append({
        'business_id': biz['business_id'],
        'state': state,
        'wifi_available': wifi_available,
        'wifi_value': wifi_value
    })

# Print results
print('Processing results:')
for r in results:
    print(f"  {r['business_id']}: state={r['state']}, WiFi={r['wifi_available']} ({r['wifi_value']})")

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(results, default=str))

val_args.code:
import json
import re

# Load all businesses
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:44']

# Debug print
print('Total businesses:', len(businesses))

# Process each business to extract state and WiFi
results = []
regex = re.compile(r'in [^,]+, ([A-Z]{2}),')

for biz in businesses:
    # Extract state
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    state_match = regex.search(desc)
    state = state_match.group(1) if state_match else 'Unknown'
    
    # Check WiFi
    attrs = biz.get('attributes', {})
    wifi_available = False
    wifi_value = None
    
    if isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi_value = attrs.get('WiFi', '')
        if wifi_value and 'no' not in wifi_value.lower():
            wifi_available = True
    
    results.append({
        'business_id': biz['business_id'],
        'state': state,
        'wifi_available': wifi_available,
        'wifi_value': wifi_value
    })

# Print results
print('Processing results:')
for r in results:
    print(f"  {r['business_id']}: state={r['state']}, WiFi={r['wifi_available']} ({r['wifi_value']})")

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(results, default=str))

var_functions.query_db:5.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

import json
import re

# Load businesses
businesses_file = var_functions.query_db:54

# Read the file if it's a file path
if isinstance(businesses_file, str) and businesses_file.endswith('.json'):
    with open(businesses_file, 'r') as f:
        businesses = json.load(f)
else:
    businesses = businesses_file

print('Total businesses loaded:', len(businesses))
print('Type of businesses:', type(businesses))

# Check for any businesses with WiFi
wifi_available = []

var_functions.query_db:5.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

val_args.code:
import json
import re

# Load businesses
businesses_file = var_functions.query_db:54

# Read the file if it's a file path
if isinstance(businesses_file, str) and businesses_file.endswith('.json'):
    with open(businesses_file, 'r') as f:
        businesses = json.load(f)
else:
    businesses = businesses_file

print('Total businesses loaded:', len(businesses))
print('Type of businesses:', type(businesses))

# Check for any businesses with WiFi
wifi_available = []
for biz in businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes', {})
    if isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi = attrs.get('WiFi', '')
        print(f"Business {biz['business_id']}: WiFi={wifi}")
        if wifi and wifi != "u'no'":
            wifi_available.append(biz)

print(f"\nBusinesses with WiFi available: {len(wifi_available)}")

if wifi_available:
    for biz in wifi_available:
        print(f"  - {biz['business_id']}: {biz['name']}")
else:
    print('No businesses offer WiFi in this dataset')

var_functions.query_db:5.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import defaultdict

# Load all businesses
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:58']

print('Total businesses in dataset:', len(businesses))

# Extract state and check WiFi for each business
state_counts = defaultdict(int)
businesses_with_wifi = []
state_pattern = re.compile(r'in [^,]+, ([A-Z]{2}),')

for biz in businesses:
    # Extract state from description
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    state_match = state_pattern.search(desc)
    state = state_match.group(1) if state_match else 'Unknown'
    
    # Check if WiFi is available (not 'no')
    attrs = biz.get('attributes', {})
    wifi_available = False
    
    if isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi = attrs.get('WiFi', '')
        if wifi and "u'no'" not in str(wifi):
            wifi_available = True
            businesses_with_wifi.append({
                'business_id': biz['business_id'],
                'state': state,
                'wifi_value': wifi
            })
    
    if wifi_available:
        state_counts[state] += 1

print('Businesses with WiFi available:', len(businesses_with_wifi))
print('State counts:', dict(state_counts))
print('Businesses with WiFi details:', businesses_with_wifi)

# Find state with most WiFi businesses
if state_counts:
    max_state = max(state_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1])
    print('State with most WiFi businesses:', max_state)
else:
    print('No businesses with WiFi found')

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_businesses': len(businesses),
    'wifi_businesses_count': len(businesses_with_wifi),
    'state_counts': dict(state_counts),
    'businesses_with_wifi': businesses_with_wifi
}, default=str))

var_functions.query_db:5.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

val_args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import defaultdict

# Load all businesses
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:58']

print('Total businesses in dataset:', len(businesses))

# Extract state and check WiFi for each business
state_counts = defaultdict(int)
businesses_with_wifi = []
state_pattern = re.compile(r'in [^,]+, ([A-Z]{2}),')

for biz in businesses:
    # Extract state from description
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    state_match = state_pattern.search(desc)
    state = state_match.group(1) if state_match else 'Unknown'
    
    # Check if WiFi is available (not 'no')
    attrs = biz.get('attributes', {})
    wifi_available = False
    
    if isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi = attrs.get('WiFi', '')
        if wifi and "u'no'" not in str(wifi):
            wifi_available = True
            businesses_with_wifi.append({
                'business_id': biz['business_id'],
                'state': state,
                'wifi_value': wifi
            })
    
    if wifi_available:
        state_counts[state] += 1

print('Businesses with WiFi available:', len(businesses_with_wifi))
print('State counts:', dict(state_counts))
print('Businesses with WiFi details:', businesses_with_wifi)

# Find state with most WiFi businesses
if state_counts:
    max_state = max(state_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1])
    print('State with most WiFi businesses:', max_state)
else:
    print('No businesses with WiFi found')

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_businesses': len(businesses),
    'wifi_businesses_count': len(businesses_with_wifi),
    'state_counts': dict(state_counts),
    'businesses_with_wifi': businesses_with_wifi
}, default=str))

var_functions.query_db:5.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

args.code:
import json

# Load all businesses
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:58']

# Check all WiFi attributes
print('WiFi status for all businesses:')
for biz in businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes', {})
    business_id = biz['business_id']
    name = biz.get('name', 'Unknown')
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    
    if isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi = attrs.get('WiFi', 'NOT_SPECIFIED')
    else:
        wifi = f'ATTRIBUTES_STRING: {attrs}'
    
    print(f"{business_id} ({name}): WiFi={wifi}")
    print(f"  Description snippet: {desc[:80]}...")
    print()

# Count businesses with WiFi
wifi_businesses = []
for biz in businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes', {})
    if isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi = attrs.get('WiFi', '')
        # Only count if WiFi is explicitly not 'no'
        if wifi and 'no' not in wifi:
            wifi_businesses.append(biz)

print(f'Total businesses with WiFi available: {len(wifi_businesses)}')

# Show which IDs they would map to in review table
print('\nBusiness ID mapping for review table:')
for biz in businesses:
    business_id = biz['business_id']
    review_ref = business_id.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_')
    print(f"{business_id} -> {review_ref}")

If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

val_args.code:
import json

# Load all businesses
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:58']

# Check all WiFi attributes
print('WiFi status for all businesses:')
for biz in businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes', {})
    business_id = biz['business_id']
    name = biz.get('name', 'Unknown')
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    
    if isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi = attrs.get('WiFi', 'NOT_SPECIFIED')
    else:
        wifi = f'ATTRIBUTES_STRING: {attrs}'
    
    print(f"{business_id} ({name}): WiFi={wifi}")
    print(f"  Description snippet: {desc[:80]}...")
    print()

# Count businesses with WiFi
wifi_businesses = []
for biz in businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes', {})
    if isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi = attrs.get('WiFi', '')
        # Only count if WiFi is explicitly not 'no'
        if wifi and 'no' not in wifi:
            wifi_businesses.append(biz)

print(f'Total businesses with WiFi available: {len(wifi_businesses)}')

# Show which IDs they would map to in review table
print('\nBusiness ID mapping for review table:')
for biz in businesses:
    business_id = biz['business_id']
    review_ref = business_id.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_')
    print(f"{business_id} -> {review_ref}")

var_functions.query_db:5.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

[{"business_ref": "businessref_54", "avg_rating": "3.5", "review_count": "10"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_55", "avg_rating": "4.918918918918919", "review_count": "37"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_26", "avg_rating": "1.7083333333333333", "review_count": "24"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_51", "avg_rating": "3.9714285714285715", "review_count": "35"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_20", "avg_rating": "3.2142857142857144", "review_count": "42"}]

args.query: SELECT business_ref, AVG(CAST(rating AS FLOAT)) as avg_rating, COUNT(*) as review_count FROM review WHERE business_ref IN ('businessref_54', 'businessref_26', 'businessref_51', 'businessref_55', 'businessref_20') GROUP BY business_ref

exec_args.sql: SELECT business_ref, AVG(CAST(rating AS FLOAT)) as avg_rating, COUNT(*) as review_count FROM review WHERE business_ref IN ('businessref_54', 'businessref_26', 'businessref_51', 'businessref_55', 'businessref_20') GROUP BY business_ref
